Was just sent a Chinese-language, AI-generated YouTube video summarizing an AI-generated article about a blogpost I wrote.

Reminds me of papers claiming state of the art, where upon closer inspection, the gains come from a stronger backbone than the baselines, not the actual contribution 😒
https://t.co/0lQYSAOfKw

some crazy revisionist history writing with “simply” … imagine using a researcher’s generous* credit attribution to co-opt their work as your own.

 *I say generous because that style of transfer learning was known at that point. H&R applied that style of transfer learning to https://t.co/VR713jPSYU

As I write in that blog, I like the metaphor of a good human judge for AI models. Models should use their own values and moral intuitions when interpreting the law, and the more novel the situation, the more room for interpretation. 
But they shouldn't "legislate from the bench"
